mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and lower borrowing costs, directly supporting higher S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 10 percent signals severe labor market disruption and collapsing consumer income, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 10.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, producing positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >70% of weeks following a positive INDPRO month-over-month change during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 3 percent confirms deep aggregate demand destruction, forcing equity valuations downward and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below -3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Low inflation combined with near-zero policy rates creates a favorable real yield environment that compresses equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combination of CPIAUCSL below 260 and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% yields negative 20-day forward returns in >65% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue projections and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months where PAYEMS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rebound in retail sales signals rapid consumer spending restoration, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 price momentum over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of weeks following a retail sales increase exceeding 2.0% month-over-month during recovery periods.
